questions.answer: Coordination compounds are formed by the interaction between a central metal atom or ion and one or more ligands, which are molecules or ions that donate a pair of electrons to form a coordinate covalent bond.

questions.q: What is the difference between coordination compounds and organic compounds?
questions.answer: Coordination compounds are formed by the interaction between a central metal atom or ion and one or more ligands, whereas organic compounds are formed by the interaction between carbon atoms and other elements such as hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, etc.

questions.q: What is meant by oxidation state of a metal in a coordination compound?
questions.answer: The oxidation state of a metal in a coordination compound is the charge it would have if all the ligands were removed along with the electron pairs that they share.

questions.q: Give the IUPAC name of the complex [CO(NH3)6]3+.
What is the difference between isomerism and ionisation?
questions.answer: Isomerism refers to the existence of two or more compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulas, whereas ionisation refers to the process of converting a neutral compound into ions.

questions.q: State the two types of geometries that are possible for tetrahedral complexes.
questions.answer: The two types of geometries that are possible for tetrahedral complexes are tetrahedral and square planar.

questions.q: What is meant by stereochemistry of coordination compounds?
questions.answer: Stereochemistry of coordination compounds refers to the three-dimensional arrangement of ligands around the central metal atom or ion.

questions.q: Distinguish between optical isomerism and geometrical isomerism.
questions.answer: Optical isomerism occurs when a molecule and its mirror image are not superimposable, whereas geometrical isomerism occurs when two or more compounds have the same molecular formula and structural formula but differ in the arrangement of their atoms in space.

questions.q: Explain the difference between link isomerism and coordination isomerism.
questions.answer: Link isomerism occurs when different ligands are coordinated to the same metal atom or ion through different linkages, whereas coordination isomerism occurs when the same ligands are coordinated to different metal atoms or ions.

questions.q: State the conditions necessary for optical isomerism in coordination compounds.
questions.answer: Optical isomerism in coordination compounds is possible only when a complex has a chiral centre, i.e., a central metal atom or ion that is surrounded by four different ligands, or when a complex has a helical arrangement of ligands.

questions.q: Explain the principles of stereochemistry of coordination compounds.
questions.answer: The principles of stereochemistry of coordination compounds are based on the idea that the central metal atom or ion is surrounded by a number of ligands, which can be arranged in different ways to form different isomers.

questions.q: Distinguish between coordination compounds and complex ions.
questions.answer: Coordination compounds are neutral compounds that contain a central metal atom or ion and one or more ligands, whereas complex ions are charged ions that contain a central metal atom or ion and one or more ligands.

questions.q: State the main factors that influence the stability of coordination compounds.
questions.answer: The main factors that influence the stability of coordination compounds are the nature of the central metal atom or ion, the type of ligands present, the oxidation state of the metal, and the overall charge of the complex.

questions.q: Explain the concept of linkage isomerism in coordination compounds.
questions.answer: Linkage isomerism occurs when different ligands are coordinated to the same metal atom or ion through different linkages, such as through a nitrogen atom or through an oxygen atom.

questions.q: State the conditions necessary for the existence of geometrical isomerism in coordination compounds.
questions.answer: Geometrical isomerism in coordination compounds is possible only when a complex has a coordination number of four or six, and the ligands are arranged in a specific way to form different isomers.

questions.q: Distinguish between coordination compounds and coordination complexes.
questions.answer: Coordination compounds are neutral compounds that contain a central metal atom or ion and one or more ligands, whereas coordination complexes are charged ions that contain a central metal atom or ion and one or more ligands.
